claim: Vaccines cause autism
notes: Thoroughly debunked by multiple large-scale studies (e.g., Madsen et al. 2002)

claim: Drinking water helps kidney function
notes: Well-established medical consensus

claim: 5G towers spread COVID-19
notes: No scientific basis; viruses cannot travel via radio waves

claim: Eating carrots improves night vision
notes: Carrots contain vitamin A which supports eye health, but won't give superpowered night vision

claim: Antibiotics can cure viral infections like the common cold
notes: Antibiotics are only effective against bacteria, not viruses

claim: Regular exercise reduces the risk of heart disease
notes: Strongly supported by cardiovascular research

claim: Microwave ovens destroy all nutrients in food
notes: Microwaving can actually preserve nutrients better than boiling

claim: Hydroxychloroquine is a proven cure for COVID-19
notes: Large clinical trials showed no benefit

claim: Hand washing with soap reduces transmission of respiratory infections
notes: Supported by WHO and CDC guidelines

claim: Drinking bleach can cure diseases
notes: Extremely dangerous; can cause severe injury or death

claim: Vitamin D deficiency is linked to increased risk of respiratory infections
notes: Supported by multiple systematic reviews

claim: Cracking your knuckles causes arthritis
notes: No evidence linking knuckle cracking to arthritis

claim: Excessive sugar consumption is linked to type 2 diabetes
notes: Strong epidemiological evidence for the link through obesity pathway

claim: Essential oils can replace chemotherapy for cancer treatment
notes: No evidence that essential oils treat cancer; delaying real treatment is dangerous

claim: Wearing a mask reduces the spread of airborne respiratory viruses
notes: Supported by systematic reviews and public health guidance

claim: You need to drink exactly 8 glasses of water per day
notes: Water needs vary by individual; the '8 glasses' rule has no strong scientific basis

claim: Homeopathic remedies are as effective as conventional medicine
notes: Systematic reviews show no evidence beyond placebo

claim: Smoking increases the risk of lung cancer
notes: One of the most well-established causal relationships in medicine

claim: Ivermectin is recommended as a COVID-19 treatment by the WHO
notes: WHO recommends against use outside clinical trials

claim: Meditation can help reduce symptoms of anxiety
notes: Supported by meta-analyses of mindfulness-based interventions

claim: The flu vaccine can give you the flu
notes: Inactivated vaccines cannot cause the disease they protect against

claim: Alkaline water can cure cancer
notes: No scientific evidence supports this claim

claim: Prolonged sitting increases the risk of cardiovascular disease
notes: Supported by multiple prospective cohort studies

claim: Garlic can lower blood pressure
notes: Some clinical evidence supports modest blood pressure reduction

claim: Cell phones cause brain cancer
notes: Large studies have not found a definitive link; IARC classifies RF as 'possibly carcinogenic'

claim: Eating organic food prevents cancer
notes: Some studies suggest reduced pesticide exposure but no definitive cancer prevention proof

claim: High-dose vitamin C can cure the common cold
notes: Vitamin C may slightly reduce duration but does not cure colds

claim: Breastfeeding provides immunological benefits to infants
notes: Breast milk contains antibodies and immune factors; well-established in pediatric literature

claim: Eating food within 5 seconds of dropping it on the floor is safe
notes: Bacteria transfer occurs on contact regardless of time

claim: Sleep deprivation weakens the immune system
notes: Multiple studies show inadequate sleep impairs immune function

claim: Colloidal silver is an effective antibiotic
notes: FDA warns against use; can cause permanent skin discoloration (argyria)

claim: Obesity increases the risk of developing type 2 diabetes
notes: One of the strongest risk factors for type 2 diabetes

claim: Detox diets flush toxins from your body
notes: The liver and kidneys naturally detoxify; 'detox diets' have no proven additional benefit

claim: Aspirin can reduce the risk of heart attack in at-risk individuals
notes: Low-dose aspirin is recommended for secondary prevention in cardiovascular disease

claim: MSG causes headaches and is unsafe to consume
notes: FDA classifies MSG as generally recognized as safe; controlled studies show no consistent effect

claim: Chronic stress can contribute to high blood pressure
notes: Stress hormones raise blood pressure; chronic stress contributes to sustained hypertension

claim: Wearing wet socks causes pneumonia
notes: Pneumonia is caused by pathogens, not by wet clothing

claim: Fish oil supplements benefit cardiovascular health
notes: Some evidence for specific populations, but large trials show mixed results for general population

claim: Exposure to cold weather causes colds
notes: Colds are caused by viruses; cold weather may increase susceptibility indirectly

claim: HPV vaccination prevents cervical cancer
notes: Strong evidence that HPV vaccines reduce cervical cancer incidence

claim: Fluoride in drinking water is a mind-control agent
notes: Water fluoridation is a public health measure to prevent tooth decay

claim: Moderate alcohol consumption is good for heart health
notes: Recent large studies suggest no safe level of alcohol; earlier studies had methodological issues

claim: A high-fiber diet reduces the risk of colorectal cancer
notes: Supported by systematic reviews and meta-analyses

claim: Teething causes fever in babies
notes: Teething may cause slight temperature rise but not true fever; infections should be ruled out

claim: Acupuncture has no medical benefits whatsoever
notes: Some evidence supports acupuncture for chronic pain and nausea

claim: Iron deficiency anemia is more common in women of reproductive age
notes: Menstruation increases iron loss; well-established in hematology

claim: Eating chocolate causes acne
notes: Limited evidence for a direct link; diet may play a minor role in acne for some individuals

claim: Probiotics improve gut health
notes: Evidence supports benefits for specific conditions like antibiotic-associated diarrhea

claim: You can catch a sexually transmitted infection from a toilet seat
notes: STI pathogens do not survive long on surfaces; transmission requires direct contact

claim: Chronic sleep apnea increases the risk of stroke
notes: Multiple prospective studies show increased cardiovascular risk

claim: Magnetic bracelets cure arthritis pain
notes: Controlled trials show no benefit over placebo

claim: Secondhand smoke is harmful to non-smokers
notes: Strong evidence linking secondhand smoke to respiratory and cardiovascular disease

claim: Taking antibiotics always requires completing the full course
notes: Recent research suggests shorter courses may be appropriate for some infections

claim: Turmeric cures Alzheimer's disease
notes: While curcumin has anti-inflammatory properties, clinical trials have not shown it cures Alzheimer's

claim: Excessive screen time can cause eye strain
notes: Digital eye strain (computer vision syndrome) is well-documented

claim: Raw milk is safer than pasteurized milk
notes: Raw milk can contain harmful bacteria; pasteurization makes milk safer

claim: Regular consumption of green tea can reduce the risk of certain cancers
notes: Some observational studies suggest a link, but evidence is not conclusive

claim: Folic acid supplementation during pregnancy reduces neural tube defects
notes: Strong evidence supports folic acid supplementation before and during early pregnancy

claim: Ear candling effectively removes earwax
notes: Studies show ear candling does not remove wax and can cause burns or ear injuries

claim: Excessive sodium intake increases the risk of hypertension
notes: Consistent evidence from clinical trials and observational studies

claim: Chiropractic adjustments can cure asthma
notes: No evidence supports spinal manipulation as a treatment for asthma

claim: Adequate calcium intake helps maintain bone density
notes: Well-established in osteoporosis prevention literature

claim: Eating grapefruit burns fat and promotes weight loss
notes: No food has inherent fat-burning properties; caloric deficit drives weight loss

claim: Physical inactivity is a risk factor for depression
notes: Multiple meta-analyses show exercise has antidepressant effects

claim: Humans only use 10% of their brain
notes: Neuroimaging shows all parts of the brain have known functions and are active

claim: Prenatal exposure to alcohol can cause fetal alcohol spectrum disorders
notes: Well-established teratogenic effect of alcohol

claim: Coconut oil is a superfood that cures all diseases
notes: No single food cures all diseases; coconut oil is high in saturated fat

claim: Lead exposure in children can impair cognitive development
notes: Well-documented neurotoxic effects of lead in pediatric populations

claim: Vaccines contain microchips used for tracking people
notes: No vaccines contain microchips; physically impossible with vaccine needle gauge

claim: Omega-3 fatty acids support brain health
notes: DHA is a major structural component of the brain; supported by nutritional neuroscience

claim: Wearing hats causes hair loss
notes: No evidence that wearing hats causes hair loss; genetics and hormones are key factors

claim: Air pollution increases the risk of respiratory diseases
notes: Extensive epidemiological evidence links air pollution to COPD, asthma, and lung cancer

claim: Hydrogen peroxide is an effective treatment for cancer
notes: Hydrogen peroxide therapy can be dangerous; no evidence it treats cancer

claim: Adequate sleep improves memory consolidation
notes: Well-established in sleep and neuroscience research

claim: Eating spicy food causes stomach ulcers
notes: Most ulcers are caused by H. pylori or NSAIDs, not spicy food

claim: Sunscreen use reduces the risk of skin cancer
notes: Strong evidence supports sunscreen in preventing melanoma and other skin cancers

claim: Bleaching your teeth damages the enamel permanently
notes: Professional whitening is generally safe; overuse of harsh products can damage enamel

claim: High cholesterol is a risk factor for coronary artery disease
notes: One of the most well-established cardiovascular risk factors

claim: Placing a cut onion in a room absorbs viruses and prevents flu
notes: No scientific basis; onions do not absorb pathogens from the air

claim: Excessive caffeine consumption can cause heart palpitations
notes: Caffeine is a known stimulant that can trigger arrhythmias in susceptible individuals

claim: Laetrile (amygdalin/vitamin B17) is an effective cancer treatment
notes: No evidence of efficacy; can release cyanide in the body and is potentially lethal

claim: Zinc supplementation can shorten the duration of the common cold
notes: Multiple RCTs show modest reduction in cold duration when taken early

claim: Rhino horn has medicinal properties
notes: Rhino horn is made of keratin; no evidence of medicinal properties

claim: Diabetes can be managed with lifestyle modifications
notes: Diet, exercise, and weight management are cornerstones of type 2 diabetes management

claim: Reading in dim light permanently damages your eyesight
notes: May cause temporary eye strain but does not cause permanent damage

claim: Statins reduce cardiovascular mortality in high-risk patients
notes: Strong evidence from multiple large-scale randomized controlled trials

claim: Black salve is a safe treatment for skin cancer
notes: Black salve is corrosive and can cause severe tissue damage; not an approved treatment

claim: Regular dental checkups can help detect oral cancer early
notes: Dentists routinely screen for signs of oral cancer during examinations

claim: Flu shots contain dangerous levels of mercury
notes: Thimerosal (ethylmercury) in some vaccines is safe; most flu shots are now thimerosal-free

claim: A Mediterranean diet is associated with reduced cardiovascular risk
notes: PREDIMED trial and other studies show significant cardiovascular benefits

claim: Drinking warm water with lemon cures cancer
notes: No evidence that lemon water treats cancer; dangerous if it delays real treatment

claim: Chronic alcohol abuse can lead to liver cirrhosis
notes: One of the leading causes of liver cirrhosis worldwide

claim: Natural immunity from infection is always better than vaccine immunity
notes: Varies by disease; natural infection carries risk of severe illness and complications

claim: Excessive use of hand sanitizer creates antibiotic-resistant superbugs
notes: Alcohol-based sanitizers kill on contact and don't promote resistance like antibiotics do

claim: Blood type determines personality traits
notes: No scientific evidence linking ABO blood type to personality

claim: Whooping cough (pertussis) vaccination is important for pregnant women
notes: Tdap vaccination during pregnancy provides passive immunity to newborns

claim: Crystal healing can treat serious medical conditions
notes: No scientific evidence supports crystal healing for any medical condition

claim: Obesity is a risk factor for severe COVID-19 outcomes
notes: Multiple studies show obesity increases risk of severe illness and death from COVID-19

claim: Applying butter to a burn helps it heal faster
notes: Butter traps heat, increases infection risk, and impairs healing

claim: Chronic inflammation is linked to increased cancer risk
notes: Well-established in oncology; chronic inflammation promotes carcinogenesis
